title: Small Models Have Big Power! Cisco Open Sources Antares AI to Help Enterprises Accurately Identify Code Vulnerabilities
excerpt: The cybersecurity defense has welcomed a new intelligent assistant, as Cisco officially open-sourced the small AI model family Antares on Tuesday. This model is
content_html:
The cybersecurity defense has welcomed a new intelligent assistant, as Cisco officially open-sourced the small AI model family Antares on Tuesday. This model is specifically designed for software code repository investigations, accurately locating known security vulnerabilities in the code.

Compared to large AI models that often require significant investment, Antares demonstrates remarkable efficiency while maintaining high accuracy. Testing shows it can scan 500 code repositories in just 15 minutes, with a cost of less than $1.

Focused on local privacy protection, learning from hackers' approaches to accurately search

A key advantage is that this small model supports running locally within enterprises, completely eliminating the need to upload sensitive source code to third-party service providers. Its training logic resembles that of an experienced security investigator, enabling it to autonomously trace clues within massive codebases.

To address potential risks of misuse, Cisco has collaborated with relevant organizations to establish a download verification mechanism. Currently, the Antares-350M and 1B versions are available on open-source platforms for verified defenders.

Promoting the open-source security ecosystem, industry collaboration strengthens network defenses

The more powerful 3B version is not yet publicly released; Cisco plans to integrate it directly into its own security product line. Cisco is exploring the establishment of an industry alliance to further expand cooperation in the field of open-source AI security tools.

As multiple companies have launched related tools, industrial collaboration in the open-source security field is gradually deepening. This efficient and low-cost AI protection tool will build a more solid defense for cybersecurity in the digital age.
content_text: The cybersecurity defense has welcomed a new intelligent assistant, as Cisco officially open-sourced the small AI model family Antares on Tuesday. This model is specifically designed for software code repository investigations, accurately locating known security vulnerabilities in the code. Compared to large AI models that often require significant investment, Antares demonstrates remarkable efficiency while maintaining high accuracy. Testing shows it can scan 500 code repositories in just 15 minutes, with a cost of less than $1. Focused on local privacy protection, learning from hackers' approaches to accurately search A key advantage is that this small model supports running locally within enterprises, completely eliminating the need to upload sensitive source code to third-party service providers. Its training logic resembles that of an experienced security investigator, enabling it to autonomously trace clues within massive codebases. To address potential risks of misuse, Cisco has collaborated with relevant organizations to establish a download verification mechanism. Currently, the Antares-350M and 1B versions are available on open-source platforms for verified defenders. Promoting the open-source security ecosystem, industry collaboration strengthens network defenses The more powerful 3B version is not yet publicly released; Cisco plans to integrate it directly into its own security product line. Cisco is exploring the establishment of an industry alliance to further expand cooperation in the field of open-source AI security tools. As multiple companies have launched related tools, industrial collaboration in the open-source security field is gradually deepening. This efficient and low-cost AI protection tool will build a more solid defense for cybersecurity in the digital age.

content_html_zh:
随着思科周二正式开源小型人工智能模型系列 Antares，网络安全防御迎来了新的智能助手。 该模型专为软件代码存储库调查而设计，可准确定位代码中已知的安全漏洞。

与通常需要大量投资的大型人工智能模型相比，Antares 在保持高精度的同时表现出显着的效率。 测试表明，它可以在短短 15 分钟内扫描 500 个代码存储库，成本不到 1 美元。

专注本地隐私保护，借鉴黑客手段精准搜索

一个关键优势是，这种小型模型支持在企业内部本地运行，完全无需将敏感源代码上传到第三方服务提供商。 它的训练逻辑类似于经验丰富的安全调查员的训练逻辑，使其能够在大量代码库中自主追踪线索。

为了解决潜在的滥用风险，思科与相关组织合作建立了下载验证机制。 目前，Antares-350M 和 1B 版本可在开源平台上供经过验证的防御者使用。

推动开源安全生态，行业协作强化网络防御

更强大的3B版本尚未公开发布；思科计划将其直接集成到自己的安全产品线中。 思科正在探索建立产业联盟，以进一步扩大在开源AI安全工具领域的合作。

随着多家公司推出相关工具，开源安全领域的产业合作正在逐步深化。 这种高效、低成本的人工智能防护工具，将为数字时代的网络安全筑起更加坚固的防线。
